import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut current = ""
  mut has = false
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(c)
      if c == 32 or c == 9 or c == 10 or c == 13
        if has
          match string.parse_int(current)
          when Some(v)
            result = list.push(result, v)
          when None
            ()
          end
          current = ""
          has = false
        end
      else
        current = "#{current}#{string.from_byte(c)}"
        has = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    match string.parse_int(current)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn find_pair(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<String>
  let n = xs.len()
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut j = i + 1
    while j < n
      match xs.get(i)
      when Some(a)
        match xs.get(j)
        when Some(b)
          if a + b == t
            return Some("found=#{i},#{j}")
          end
        when None
          ()
        end
      when None
        ()
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  None
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let nums = parse_ints(line1)
  let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
  match find_pair(nums, target)
  when Some(msg)
    print(msg)
  when None
    print("none")
  end
end
